message:
feat(renderer): implement double-buffered rendering with diff detection

This commit adds the renderer subsystem with double buffering for
flicker-free updates, cell-level diff detection for minimal output,
hit testing for mouse event dispatch, and syntax highlighting support.

src/renderer/mod.rs - Renderer:
Renderer struct:
- front_buffer: Currently displayed buffer
- back_buffer: Drawing target buffer
- terminal: Terminal<Stdout> for output
- hit_grid: HitGrid for mouse dispatch
- link_pool: LinkPool for hyperlinks
- force_redraw: bool to skip diff
- stats: RenderStats for metrics

RendererOptions:
- width, height: Initial dimensions
- color_mode: ColorMode (TrueColor/256/16)
- enable_mouse: bool
- enable_sync_output: bool

Construction:
- new(width, height) -> Result<Renderer>
- with_options(options) -> Result<Renderer>
- Enters alt screen, hides cursor, enables mouse

Operations:
- buffer() -> &mut OptimizedBuffer: Get back buffer
- present() -> Result<()>: Render and swap buffers
- present_force() -> Result<()>: Full redraw
- resize(w, h): Handle terminal resize
- invalidate(): Mark for full redraw

Cursor Control:
- set_cursor_position(x, y)
- set_cursor_visible(visible)
- set_cursor_style(shape, blink)

Hit Testing:
- register_hit_area(id, x, y, w, h)
- clear_hit_areas()
- hit_test(x, y) -> Option<HitAreaId>

Debug:
- render_stats_overlay(): Show FPS and cell count
- memory_usage() -> usize: Estimate buffer memory

Cleanup:
- Drop impl: Restores cursor, exits alt screen, disables mouse

RenderStats:
- frame_count: Total frames rendered
- cells_updated: Cells changed this frame
- frame_time_us: Microseconds for present()
- fps: Calculated frames per second

src/renderer/diff.rs - Diff Detection:
compute_diff(old, new) -> DiffResult:
- Compares buffers cell by cell
- Returns list of changed cells with positions
- Groups adjacent changes into dirty regions

DiffResult:
- changed_cells: Vec<(x, y, Cell)>
- dirty_regions: Vec<Rect> for optimization
- total_changed: usize count

Algorithm:
  for y in 0..height:
    for x in 0..width:
      if old.get(x, y) != new.get(x, y):
        changed_cells.push((x, y, new.get(x, y)))

Optimization Strategies:
- Skip identical cells (most common case)
- Batch adjacent changes by row
- Early exit if buffers match

Performance:
- O(w*h) worst case (full redraw)
- O(changed) typical case
- 80x24 = 1920 comparisons max

src/renderer/hitgrid.rs - Hit Testing:
HitGrid struct:
- width, height: Grid dimensions
- grid: Vec<Option<HitAreaId>>
- areas: Vec<HitArea> registered areas
- scissor_stack: Clipping for hit areas

HitArea:
- id: HitAreaId (unique identifier)
- x, y, width, height: Bounding box
- priority: u8 for overlap resolution

Operations:
- register(id, x, y, w, h, priority)
- unregister(id)
- clear(): Remove all areas
- test(x, y) -> Option<HitAreaId>

Scissor Support:
- push_scissor(rect): Clip subsequent registrations
- pop_scissor(): Restore previous clip
- Hit areas clipped to scissor on registration

Priority Resolution:
- Higher priority wins on overlap
- Equal priority: later registration wins

Use Cases:
- Clickable UI elements
- Hover detection
- Scrollbar regions
- Tab headers

src/highlight/mod.rs - Module Exports:
- SyntaxStyle, SyntaxStyleRegistry
- Re-exports for syntax highlighting

src/highlight/syntax.rs - Syntax Highlighting:
SyntaxStyle struct:
- name: String (e.g., "keyword", "string")
- style: Style to apply
- priority: u8 for overlap

SyntaxStyleRegistry:
- styles: HashMap<String, SyntaxStyle>
- Lookup by name for efficient application

Operations:
- new() -> SyntaxStyleRegistry
- register(name, style, priority)
- get(name) -> Option<&SyntaxStyle>
- clear()

Integration with TextBuffer:
- TextBuffer holds Option<Arc<SyntaxStyleRegistry>>
- Syntax highlighter produces segment positions
- Segments reference registry styles
- Priority-based composition with other highlights

Architecture Notes:
- Registry shared via Arc for efficiency
- Separate from parsing (bring your own parser)
- Works with tree-sitter, regex, or manual

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
